name: Harvard University
description: The oldest university in the United States, Harvard has shaped presidents, Nobel laureates, and global leaders for nearly four centuries. Its 12 degree-granting schools span Cambridge and Boston, with a $50 billion endowment funding world-class research and generous financial aid.

name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
description: MIT is the world's premier science and technology university. Its hands-on, project-driven culture has produced 97 Nobel laureates and countless industry-defining companies. Five schools cover science, engineering, architecture, management, and humanities.

name: Stanford University
description: Located in the heart of Silicon Valley, Stanford is a global hub for entrepreneurship and innovation. Its alumni have founded Google, Netflix, and hundreds of other companies. Seven schools and a 8,000-acre campus make it one of the world's most dynamic research institutions.

name: Columbia University
description: One of the oldest universities in America, Columbia sits in the heart of Manhattan. Its Ivy League prestige, combined with New York City access, attracts elite students worldwide. Columbia is especially renowned for its law school, journalism programme, and business school.

name: University of Oxford
description: The oldest English-speaking university in the world, Oxford has educated 28 British Prime Ministers, 30 world leaders, and 72 Nobel laureates. Its collegiate system, tutorial teaching method, and ancient libraries create an unparalleled intellectual environment.

name: University of Cambridge
description: Cambridge is consistently ranked among the top three universities in the world. With 121 Nobel laureates affiliated to its colleges, it leads in science, mathematics, and engineering. Its 31 autonomous colleges and centuries-old traditions create a uniquely immersive academic experience.

name: Imperial College London
description: A global top-ten university focused exclusively on science, engineering, medicine, and business. Imperial's South Kensington campus sits next to world-famous museums and gives students unrivalled access to London's tech and finance industries.

name: University College London
description: UCL was the first university in England to admit students regardless of religion or gender. Today it is a global powerhouse with particular strength in life sciences, law, architecture, and the arts. Its central London location makes it a magnet for international students.

name: London School of Economics
description: The world's leading social science university, LSE is renowned for economics, political science, law, and finance. 18 Nobel laureates in economics are among its alumni. Graduates occupy senior positions in governments, international organisations, and financial institutions worldwide.

name: Technical University of Munich
description: Germany's top technical university and a global leader in engineering, natural sciences, and life sciences. TUM's entrepreneurial spirit has produced hundreds of spin-offs and start-ups. Campuses in Munich, Garching, and Weihenstephan offer state-of-the-art research facilities, and tuition is free.

name: Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich
description: One of Germany's largest and most prestigious universities, LMU Munich covers virtually every academic discipline. Its 42 faculties produce more Nobel laureates than any other German institution. The university's historic city-centre location makes it inseparable from Munich's cultural life.

name: Heidelberg University
description: Germany's oldest university and a UNESCO World Heritage city backdrop make Heidelberg an iconic academic destination. Its life sciences faculty is world-renowned, and the historic Altstadt campus attracts researchers and students from over 130 countries.

name: Humboldt University of Berlin
description: The birthplace of the modern research university concept, Humboldt-Universität has produced 57 Nobel laureates including Einstein and Planck. Located in central Berlin, it leads in humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences within the vibrant German capital.

name: Sorbonne University
description: One of the world's oldest and most celebrated universities, the Sorbonne sits at the heart of Paris's Latin Quarter. Formed by the merger of Paris VI and Paris IV, it excels in arts, humanities, sciences, and medicine, drawing students from every corner of the globe.

name: École Polytechnique
description: France's most prestigious grande école, l'X trains the country's top scientists and engineers. Its demanding entrance exam and military tradition set it apart. Alumni include three presidents and dozens of Nobel and Fields Medal winners. Located on a modern campus near Paris.

name: Sciences Po Paris
description: The world's leading institution for political science, international relations, and public affairs. Sciences Po has educated seven French presidents. Its international programmes are largely in English, and 47% of students come from outside France, creating an exceptionally cosmopolitan campus.

name: University of Melbourne
description: Australia's leading research university and a member of the prestigious Group of Eight. Located in one of the world's most liveable cities, Melbourne excels in law, medicine, education, and science. Its beautiful Parkville campus is a short tram ride from Melbourne's CBD.

name: Australian National University
description: Australia's only purpose-built national research university, the ANU was established by an Act of Parliament. It consistently ranks as Australia's best university and has close links to government and international organisations in nearby Canberra.

name: University of New South Wales
description: UNSW Sydney is renowned for its strength in engineering, business, and law. Located minutes from Bondi Beach, it ranks among the top 50 universities globally and is home to some of Australia's most cited researchers. Its Kensington campus hosts a large international student community.

name: University of Toronto
description: Canada's largest and most research-intensive university is consistently ranked in the global top 25. U of T pioneered the discovery of insulin and stem cell research. Its three campuses in Greater Toronto serve nearly 100,000 students from over 160 countries.

name: McGill University
description: Canada's most international university, with 30% of students from outside the country. McGill's bilingual Montreal setting, combined with its US-style academics and European atmosphere, makes it uniquely appealing. Its medical and law schools rank among the best in the world.

name: University of British Columbia
description: Set between mountains and ocean in one of the world's most liveable cities, UBC combines stunning natural beauty with top-ranked academics. It excels in forestry, environmental science, and business, and its Pacific location fosters deep ties with Asia-Pacific research and industry.

name: University of Tokyo
description: Japan's most prestigious university and Asia's flagship research institution. The University of Tokyo has produced 15 Prime Ministers and 10 Nobel laureates. Its Hongo campus in central Tokyo is a hub for cutting-edge research in science, engineering, and humanities.

name: Kyoto University
description: Japan's second-ranked university is celebrated for its free academic spirit and research excellence. 19 Nobel laureates are affiliated with Kyoto University. Set in Japan's ancient capital, it offers a unique blend of modern research and traditional culture.

name: Osaka University
description: One of Japan's seven former Imperial Universities, Osaka excels in medicine, engineering, and life sciences. Its collaborative culture — reflected in the motto 'live locally, grow globally' — and location in Japan's commercial heartland give students strong industry connections.

name: Peking University
description: China's most celebrated university and the birthplace of the country's modern intellectual movements. Peking University sits within the former imperial gardens in Beijing and consistently ranks as the top Chinese university for humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences.

name: Tsinghua University
description: Known as China's MIT, Tsinghua is the country's top engineering and science university. It graduates more STEM PhDs than any other institution globally. Its Beijing campus neighbours Peking University, forming the world's most powerful academic corridor in Asia.

name: Fudan University
description: One of China's most prestigious universities, Fudan is Shanghai's academic flagship and excels in medicine, economics, and international studies. Its two campuses in the heart of China's global financial centre offer students outstanding career access and international exposure.

name: Leiden University
description: The oldest university in the Netherlands, founded by William of Orange. Leiden has educated Descartes, Rembrandt (briefly), and 16 Dutch Prime Ministers. Its law, humanities, and social sciences faculties are among Europe's finest, and the historic canal city is one of Europe's most charming university towns.

name: Delft University of Technology
description: The largest and most comprehensive technical university in the Netherlands, Delft ranks in Europe's top five for engineering. Its faculties of civil, aerospace, and architecture are world-leading. The compact, bike-friendly campus city is considered one of the best student environments in the Netherlands.

name: University of Amsterdam
description: One of the world's top research universities, the UvA is spread across Amsterdam's historic canal belt. It offers the largest selection of English-taught programmes in Europe and is particularly strong in economics, media studies, psychology, and life sciences.

name: ETH Zurich
description: Europe's top technical university and a global top-ten institution. Einstein studied here; 22 Nobel laureates are affiliated with ETH Zurich. Its engineering, science, and architecture programmes are world-class, and Switzerland's innovation ecosystem makes it a gateway to some of the world's best research facilities.

name: EPFL
description: Perched on the shores of Lake Geneva, EPFL is one of Europe's most vibrant tech universities. It excels in computer science, life sciences, and engineering, and its EPFL Innovation Park hosts over 300 companies. Degrees are taught in French and English, and the campus is a magnet for international talent.

name: KTH Royal Institute of Technology
description: Sweden's largest and most prestigious technical university, KTH accounts for one-third of Sweden's technical research. Located in Stockholm, it has strong links to Scandinavia's thriving startup scene and produces graduates who power global companies like Spotify, Klarna, and Ericsson.

name: Uppsala University
description: Scandinavia's oldest university, Uppsala has educated Linnaeus, Celsius, and four Nobel Prize winners. Its vibrant student nation system and beautiful Gothic cathedral city create a uniquely immersive university experience. Uppsala excels in life sciences, humanities, and social sciences.

name: Stockholm University
description: Sweden's largest university with a strong focus on natural and social sciences. Stockholm University's leafy Frescati campus lies within Nationalstadsparken, a national urban park. The university has particularly strong programmes in climate science, mathematics, and law.

name: National University of Singapore
description: Asia's top-ranked university and a global top-ten institution. NUS offers one of the world's most vibrant multicultural campuses, combining Asian perspectives with Western academic traditions. Its law, business, and engineering schools are consistently ranked among the world's best.

name: Nanyang Technological University
description: The world's top young university, NTU is a global powerhouse in engineering, science, and business. Its stunning futuristic campus, built around a valley, hosts world-leading research centres in AI, robotics, and sustainable energy. 50% of students are international, making it one of the most diverse campuses in Asia.

name: Seoul National University
description: South Korea's most prestigious university and consistently the country's top-ranked institution. SNU's mountaintop campus in northern Seoul and its rigorous academic culture make it the dream destination for Korean students. It excels in engineering, law, medicine, and social sciences.

name: KAIST
description: Korea's MIT, KAIST is a science and technology powerhouse that transformed South Korea into a high-tech economy. Teaching is entirely in English, and the university is one of Asia's top five for engineering and computer science. Its culture of intense innovation has produced many of Korea's most successful tech entrepreneurs.

name: Yonsei University
description: One of Korea's three prestigious SKY universities, Yonsei was founded by an American missionary and is Korea's oldest private university. Its picturesque campus in Sinchon is famous for Akaraka (the legendary annual festival), and its business and medicine schools are the most sought-after in the country.

name: University of Bologna
description: The oldest university in the world and the birthplace of the European university system. La Dotta (The Learned One) has shaped Western intellectual tradition for nine centuries. Located in Bologna's vibrant historic centre, it excels in law, medicine, and arts, and is a founding institution of the Erasmus programme.

name: Sapienza University of Rome
description: The largest university in Europe by student enrolment, Sapienza sits in the heart of the Eternal City. Founded in 1303 by Pope Boniface VIII, it excels in archaeology, classics, medicine, and architecture. Its stunning city-campus near Termini Station is itself a work of fascist-era architecture.

name: Politecnico di Milano
description: Italy's best technical university and one of Europe's top five for design and engineering. Its architecture and industrial design programmes are globally ranked, and its Milan location — the world's design and fashion capital — gives students unrivalled industry access.

name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
description: One of the oldest universities in the world and the largest in Spain, the Complutense traces its origins to 1499. Its sprawling Ciudad Universitaria campus in Madrid houses 26 faculties and draws students from over 100 countries, making it a cornerstone of Spanish academic life.

name: University of Barcelona
description: Catalonia's largest university and Spain's highest-ranked public research institution. The UB's historic city-centre main building and multiple faculties spread across Barcelona's districts make it a deeply urban university with a rich cultural life and strong international exchange network.

name: University of Salamanca
description: One of the oldest universities in the world and Spain's most historic academic institution, founded in 1218. Its golden sandstone buildings and UNESCO-listed Plaza Mayor make it one of Europe's most beautiful university towns. Renowned globally for its Spanish language programmes.

name: Lomonosov Moscow State University
description: Russia's most prestigious and largest university, founded by Mikhail Lomonosov. Its imposing Stalinist-Gothic main building is one of Moscow's most iconic landmarks. MSU consistently ranks as the top university in Eastern Europe and leads in mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

name: St Petersburg University
description: Russia's second-oldest university and one of its most respected, founded by Peter the Great. Alumni include Mendeleev, Putin, and nine Nobel laureates. Set along the Neva embankment in one of Europe's most beautiful cities, it excels in law, natural sciences, and economics.

name: HSE University
description: Russia's leading institution for economics, social sciences, and law. HSE was founded in the post-Soviet era as a market-oriented alternative and rapidly became Russia's most internationally connected university. It offers many English-taught programmes and has campuses in Moscow, Saint Petersburg, and Nizhny Novgorod.

name: Indian Institute of Technology Bombay
description: India's most internationally recognised engineering institution and the top choice of India's brightest STEM students. Located on a campus surrounded by Powai Lake in Mumbai, IITB has produced founders of global companies and leaders of the world's largest tech firms.

name: Indian Institute of Technology Delhi
description: Consistently India's top-ranked institution, IIT Delhi's Hauz Khas campus in the capital hosts some of the country's finest engineers and researchers. Sundar Pichai (Google CEO) is among its alumni. The institute is a key node in India's innovation ecosystem and a gateway to global tech companies.

name: Indian Institute of Science
description: India's premier research university and Asia's leading institution for science and engineering research. Though small, IISc punches far above its weight in citations and patents. Located in the garden city of Bangalore — India's Silicon Valley — it has deep ties to the country's booming tech sector.

name: University of São Paulo
description: Latin America's largest and highest-ranked university, USP is responsible for 25% of all Brazilian scientific production. Its vast Butantã campus in São Paulo houses dozens of research institutes, museums, and hospitals. Tuition is free for all students, supported by the state of São Paulo.

name: State University of Campinas
description: Brazil's most research-intensive university per student, Unicamp consistently tops domestic rankings in science and technology. Located in Campinas — Brazil's technology capital — it has the highest ratio of master's and doctoral students in the country and generates a disproportionate share of Brazil's patents.

name: University of Buenos Aires
description: The largest university in South America and one of the ten largest in the world. UBA is tuition-free and open to all graduates of Argentine secondary education. Its 13 faculties are spread across Buenos Aires and include some of the most respected law, medicine, and social science programmes in Latin America.

name: Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute
description: Ukraine's leading technical university, named after aviation pioneer Igor Sikorsky who studied here. KPI is home to Ukraine's leading research in aerospace, electronics, and mechanical engineering. Its Science Park produces startups and inventions that drive Ukraine's growing tech sector.

name: Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv
description: Ukraine's oldest and most prestigious classical university, founded in 1834. Located on Volodymyrska Street in the heart of Kyiv, KNU excels in law, humanities, and natural sciences. It is Ukraine's primary hub for fundamental research and has educated many of the country's leading scientists and public figures.

name: V. N. Karazin Kharkiv National University
description: Ukraine's first university, founded in 1804, and consistently among its top three. The Karazin University is a historic powerhouse in physics, chemistry, and mathematics — six Nobel laureates are associated with it, including Ilya Mechnikov. Located in Kharkiv, Ukraine's second city and historic scientific capital.

name: National Technical University of Ukraine 'KPI'
description: One of Ukraine's top technical universities, with a long tradition of engineering and technology education. Located in Kyiv, it offers a wide range of programmes in engineering, electronics, and management and has strong international partnerships throughout Europe.

name: KU Leuven
description: The oldest Catholic university in the world still in operation and Belgium's highest-ranked institution. KU Leuven is Europe's most innovative university for six consecutive years according to Reuters. Its medieval Leuven campus is 25 minutes from Brussels by train, combining small-city charm with capital-city access.

name: Ghent University
description: Belgium's second-largest university and a global leader in bioscience engineering, veterinary medicine, and pharmaceutical sciences. Ghent — one of Europe's most beautiful medieval cities — has a legendary student atmosphere and is consistently ranked among Europe's top hundred universities.

name: University of Copenhagen
description: Scandinavia's largest university and the highest-ranked Nordic institution. With 9 Nobel laureates affiliated to it, UCPH leads in life sciences, medicine, and social sciences. Copenhagen's exceptional quality of life, free education (for EU students), and vibrant cycling culture make it a top student destination.

name: University of Warsaw
description: Poland's largest and most prestigious university, consistently ranked as Central Europe's top institution. Set across multiple historic campuses in Warsaw, it excels in mathematics, computer science, law, and economics. International programmes are expanding rapidly, making it a growing destination for EU exchange students.

name: Charles University
description: Central Europe's oldest university, founded by Emperor Charles IV in 1348. Charles University's 17 faculties spread across magical Prague produce more than a quarter of all Czech university graduates. It excels in medicine, law, and humanities, and Prague's medieval architecture creates an unparalleled study backdrop.
